# DeclutrMail

> DeclutrMail is a Gmail sender-control companion. Manual Archive, Later, and Delete actions show the current matching count, an available sample, and the planned Gmail changes before approval; the worker re-checks Gmail at execution, so the final affected count can change if the inbox changes in between. These actions do not create future-mail rules. Pro Autopilot applies explicitly enabled presets to future matches without per-message approval. Archive and Later can be reversed from Activity for the plan's undo window. Delete has an up-to-30-day Activity token while Gmail retains Trash; emptying Trash can end recovery sooner. A delivered unsubscribe request cannot be recalled.

Privacy is the product's boundary, stated literally: Full bodies fetched: 0.
DeclutrMail persists bounded Gmail metadata plus the account, preference, action, processor, and billing records described in its privacy policy.
It never fetches or stores a full message body, HTML, attachments, inline images, or raw MIME.
Recommendation explanations may send Anthropic bounded sender identity, deterministic verdict, confidence, rule and numerical engagement facts; no subject, Gmail Preview, or body. Pro Brief may send sender identity, subject, and Gmail Preview to Anthropic for narration; never a full body.

## Product and trust

- [Overview](https://declutrmail.com/#how-it-works): the landing-page summary of the sender-first workflow
- [How it works](https://declutrmail.com/how-it-works): Gmail remains the reading/reply surface; manual actions affect the previewed messages; enabled Pro presets handle future matches
- [Interactive inbox simulator](https://declutrmail.com/inbox-simulator): production Triage row and preview components on synthetic, browser-local sender metadata
- [Methodology](https://declutrmail.com/methodology): data boundary, deterministic recommendation cascade, action lifecycle, automation boundary, and bounded Anthropic processing
- [Pricing](https://declutrmail.com/pricing): Free, Plus, and Pro plans; paid plans carry a 30-day money-back guarantee — see the refund policy for terms
- [Sign in](https://declutrmail.com/sign-in): Google OAuth scope, metadata boundary, sync expectations, and the connect action
- [Open beta](https://declutrmail.com/beta): sign in with Google and review senders, no invite or waitlist needed
- [FAQ](https://declutrmail.com/faq): concise public questions about Gmail access, action scope, recovery, and automation
- [Help & FAQ](https://declutrmail.com/help): what is stored, how Unsubscribe works (per-sender; one-click vs manual mailto; bulk cleanup across senders on paid plans), what Archive/Later/Delete do in Gmail terms, undo windows, Autopilot Observe vs Active, disconnecting and account deletion
- [Security](https://declutrmail.com/security): metadata-only storage boundary, the single gmail.modify OAuth scope, envelope-encrypted tokens, CASA Tier 2 assessment cycle in progress, vulnerability reporting
- [Contact](https://declutrmail.com/contact): support@declutrmail.com for general support, privacy@declutrmail.com for privacy and data requests
- [Privacy policy](https://declutrmail.com/privacy): Gmail message-field disclosure, operational records, Google Limited Use disclosure, processors, retention, and deletion

## Comparisons

- [Comparison hub](https://declutrmail.com/compare): source-backed buyer guidance with visible unknown states and July 2026 verification dates
- [DeclutrMail vs Clean Email](https://declutrmail.com/vs/clean-email)
- [DeclutrMail vs Trimbox](https://declutrmail.com/vs/trimbox)
- [DeclutrMail vs SaneBox](https://declutrmail.com/vs/sanebox)
- [DeclutrMail vs Leave Me Alone](https://declutrmail.com/vs/leave-me-alone)
- [DeclutrMail vs Gmail filters](https://declutrmail.com/vs/gmail-filters)
